title: 1 Kings: Divided Kingdom
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of 1 kings: divided kingdom and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study 1 kings: divided kingdom and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: What sin did Jeroboam set up to prevent Israel from returning to Jerusalem for worship? Answer: two golden calves at Bethel and Dan, telling the people these were the gods who brought them out of Egypt.
segments.correct_answer: two golden calves at Bethel and Dan to serve as substitute worship centers
acceptable_variants: golden calves at bethel and dan echoing Aaron's sin at Sinai
he set up two calves to keep the northern tribes from going to Jerusalem
the sin of jeroboam — golden calves at bethel and dan

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The phrase used repeatedly in Kings for the golden calf worship Jeroboam established; it becomes the standard charge against every northern king and explains why the northern kingdom fell
The prophet who confronted King Ahab and the prophets of Baal on Mount Carmel in 1 Kings 18; his dramatic ministry demonstrated Yahweh's superiority over the Baal worship of Ahab's court
